_comment: Store actor references use their published Apify slugs. Runtime credentials remain operator-managed.

website_content_crawler.role: Primary self-audit crawl of the subject's OWN URLs -> clean text/markdown per page.

rag_web_browser.role: Public web search/fetch for CONTEXT (where else a self/public_figure surface is publicly referenced). Query may be a phrase OR a single URL.

_dual_use_note: rag-web-browser runs a Google search by phrase, which is a dual-use discovery technique. A free-text/name search is therefore restricted to scope=self|public_figure (the same chokepoint as username enumeration). For consented|brand|safety_evidence the query MUST be a concrete URL the operator already holds, never a name search.

wcc_defaults._compliance: respectRobotsTxtFile is forced true and CANNOT be turned off by the caller (anti-evasion). maxCrawlPages/maxCrawlDepth are capped low: a self-audit reads the subject's own surface, it does not deep-mine. saveHtmlAsFile + saveScreenshots give tamper-evident evidence handles for STIX x_integrity.

rag_defaults._compliance: maxResults capped low — context, not a dragnet. outputFormats text/markdown only (no need for raw html in the context pass).

caps._note: Hard ceilings. A caller asking above these is clamped DOWN, never up (minimum-disclosure / anti-dragnet).

blocked_host_note: Host-level blocking (login-walled private social) is NOT re-implemented here — it lives in shared/scope.js (PRIVATE_SOCIAL_HOSTS) and is enforced by reading validateScope() before any input is built. Two doors: validateScope AND this ingestion policy.

forced_overrides._why: Robots compliance is not negotiable. Even if a caller passes respectRobotsTxtFile:false, the builder overwrites it to true. This is the anti-evasion floor the proxy policy also enforces.
